signals.headline: IMF forecasts from earlier in 2026 project U
signals.raw: IMF forecasts from earlier in 2026 project U.S. **GDP** growth at 2.4% for the year (up from 2.0% in 2025), **core PCE inflation** at 2.6% by year-end (down slightly from 2.9% in 2025), and minimal Fed rate cuts (fed funds from 3.6% to 3.4%), attributing drags to **tariffs** (effective rate 7-8.5%) and trade uncertainty[1].
matched_markets: Will Powell say "Tariff inflation" during April press conference?

signals.headline: **Powell**'s December 2025 remarks called the economy "very unusual," linking most **inflation** overshoot to Trump's **
signals.raw: **Powell**'s December 2025 remarks called the economy "very unusual," linking most **inflation** overshoot to Trump's **tariffs** on goods (expected to peak Q1 2026), with non-tariff inflation near 2% and labor market weakening (official jobs data possibly overstating by ~60k/month)[2][3].
matched_markets: Will Powell say "Tariff inflation" during April press conference?

signals.headline: Fed's March 18, 2026 FOMC held rates steady, projecting one 2026 cut amid higher **inflation** forecasts, steady unemplo
signals.raw: Fed's March 18, 2026 FOMC held rates steady, projecting one 2026 cut amid higher **inflation** forecasts, steady unemployment, and tariff effects fading mid-year; Powell noted progress expected as tariff-driven goods **inflation** dissipates[4][7].
matched_markets: Will Powell say "Tariff inflation" during April press conference?
